traces.mapping_rationale: The scratch notebook explicitly previews the standard AMEX rounding trick on the wide numeric statement block before any heavier feature construction.
The raw-schema pass separates the 11 known categorical columns and casts them into an integer-like block before downstream encoding or aggregation.
The categorical-handling section inspects level concentration but keeps the final baseline at simple label-ready encoding rather than a richer custom collapse scheme.
The customer-level aggregation section groups the categorical block by `customer_ID` with `count`, `first`, `last`, and `nunique`, which is the selected representative for that equivalence group.
The same section keeps the standard AMEX numeric customer-summary aggregation scaffold without treating the alternative branches as separate required units.
After concatenating the grouped numeric and categorical summaries, the notebook flattens the multi-index columns back into a usable single-level feature matrix.
The missingness section explicitly keeps simple imputation for the better-covered numeric subset rather than a more elaborate missing-data strategy.
Explicit binary missingness flags remain in the conservative baseline because null patterns themselves are a clear AMEX signal.

notes: Conservative AMEX TC1 manual baseline trace. Each selected action is mapped back to a concrete scratch-notebook section, with the worksheet retained as the late bank-id alignment record.
